meta.architecture_style: Monorepo with three platforms: FastAPI backend, React SPA web frontend, React Native/Expo mobile app
meta.executive_summary: BedtimeApp (Tuck-in-Tales) is a monorepo generating personalized AI bedtime stories for children. The backend is FastAPI/Python using LangGraph to orchestrate multi-step AI workflows across OpenAI, Gemini, and Groq. The web frontend is a React/Vite SPA and the mobile app uses React Native/Expo with file-based routing. Authentication uses Firebase ID tokens verified by the backend, with Supabase as the primary PostgreSQL database. Real-time story and avatar generation progress is streamed to clients via Server-Sent Events.

file_placement_rules.description: One file per domain resource; receives requests and delegates to graphs/services
LangGraph StateGraph workflows for multi-step AI orchestration
Pydantic models defining domain entities and API contracts
Route-level React components; one per app screen
Custom hooks wrapping SSE connections or domain-specific state
TanStack Query hooks for mobile data fetching

naming_conventions.description: React convention: PascalCase components, camelCase hooks

naming_conventions.pattern: File-based routes use lowercase with brackets for dynamic segments
Shared domain names across backend (Pydantic) and frontend (TypeScript interfaces)
naming_conventions.description: Domain model names are mirrored across all platforms

architectural_style.title: LangGraph for AI Workflow Orchestration
architectural_style.chosen: LangGraph StateGraph with async nodes emitting SSE events
architectural_style.rationale: Multi-step AI generation (outline → pages → images) requires stateful orchestration with retries, branching, and streaming progress to the client

key_decisions.title: Server-Sent Events for real-time streaming
key_decisions.chosen: SSE via asyncio.Queue registry in utils/sse.py
key_decisions.rationale: One-way server push is sufficient for generation progress; simpler than WebSockets

key_decisions.title: Firebase Auth + Supabase for persistence
key_decisions.chosen: Firebase ID tokens verified backend-side; Supabase PostgreSQL as data store
key_decisions.rationale: Firebase handles OAuth/social login; Supabase provides RLS-protected PostgreSQL

key_decisions.title: Multi-provider AI abstraction
key_decisions.chosen: Separate client modules (openai_client, gemini_client, groq_client) selected via config
key_decisions.rationale: Enables runtime provider switching and fallback without code changes

key_decisions.title: Monorepo with concurrently
key_decisions.chosen: Single repo with root package.json orchestrating all three platforms
key_decisions.rationale: Shared domain model naming; coordinated dev startup via concurrently

key_decisions.title: Expo Router file-based routing for mobile
key_decisions.rationale: Convention-over-configuration mirrors web file-based router patterns

trade_offs.accept: Scattered Supabase query logic in routes instead of repository classes
trade_offs.benefit: Less boilerplate; acceptable at current scale

trade_offs.accept: Ad-hoc AI provider selection via if/elif chains in graphs
trade_offs.benefit: Simple; avoids over-engineering a registry for three providers

out_of_scope: Redux or Zustand global state store

components.name: API Routes
components.responsibility: FastAPI route handlers; delegates to graphs/services; returns JSON or SSE streams

key_interfaces.name: stories router
key_interfaces.description: Story creation and SSE streaming endpoint

components.name: Graph Layer
components.responsibility: LangGraph StateGraph workflows for story, avatar, and memory AI operations

key_interfaces.name: story_generator
key_interfaces.description: Compiled LangGraph for multi-page story generation

components.name: Authentication
components.responsibility: Verifies Firebase ID tokens; resolves or creates Supabase user records

key_interfaces.name: verify_firebase_token
key_interfaces.description: FastAPI Depends() callable returning UserData TypedDict

components.name: SSE Utility
components.responsibility: asyncio.Queue registry keyed by client_id; emit and stream SSE events

key_interfaces.name: sse
key_interfaces.description: Push events to queue; yield formatted SSE strings to StreamingResponse

components.name: LLM Clients
components.responsibility: Provider-specific wrappers for OpenAI, Gemini, Groq APIs

components.name: Web Frontend
components.responsibility: React SPA with pages, SSE hooks, and auth context for all app features

key_files.description: Axios HTTP client with auth token injection

components.name: Mobile Frontend
components.responsibility: Expo Router mobile app with tab navigation, TanStack Query data fetching

contracts.interface_name: Backend Pydantic models in tuck-in-tales-backend/src/models/ define API response shapes mirrored by TypeScript interfaces in tuck-in-tales-frontend/src/models/ and tuck-in-tales-mobile/src/models/
contracts.description: Backend Pydantic models in tuck-in-tales-backend/src/models/ define API response shapes mirrored by TypeScript interfaces in tuck-in-tales-frontend/src/models/ and tuck-in-tales-mobile/src/models/

contracts.interface_name: SSE event types (chunk, status, done, error) are implicit contracts between utils/sse.py and frontend hooks (useSSEStream.ts, useStoryStream.ts)
contracts.description: SSE event types (chunk, status, done, error) are implicit contracts between utils/sse.py and frontend hooks (useSSEStream.ts, useStoryStream.ts)

patterns.name: FastAPI Dependency Injection
patterns.when_to_use: All protected backend routes requiring auth or DB client
patterns.how_it_works: Depends() resolves verify_firebase_token() and get_supabase_client() per request

patterns.name: SSE Streaming
patterns.when_to_use: Long-running AI generation (story, avatar, memory analysis)
patterns.how_it_works: Graph nodes call send_sse_event(client_id, event, data); FastAPI returns StreamingResponse from sse_generator(client_id)

patterns.name: REST + Axios
patterns.when_to_use: All CRUD operations from web and mobile
patterns.how_it_works: Axios instance in api/client.ts injects Firebase Bearer token from AuthContext

patterns.name: TanStack Query (mobile)
patterns.when_to_use: Mobile data fetching with caching and background sync
patterns.how_it_works: useQuery/useMutation hooks in hooks/queries/ wrap api/client.ts calls

patterns.name: LangGraph Node Execution
patterns.how_it_works: Compiled StateGraph.stream(state) runs async nodes sequentially; nodes emit SSE events and update TypedDict state

integrations.purpose: Primary DB for all domain data; avatar image storage
Story text generation (gpt-4o-mini) and image generation (gpt-image-1)

pattern_selection_guide.rationale: Consistent auth and DB access pattern used across all existing routes

pattern_selection_guide.scenario: Long-running AI generation with frontend progress
pattern_selection_guide.pattern: LangGraph graph + SSE via sse.py + useSSEStream.ts hook

pattern_selection_guide.rationale: Caching, background sync, loading/error states handled automatically

pattern_selection.streaming_generation: LangGraph graph + send_sse_event + SSE route + useSSEStream hook
pattern_selection.mobile_data: TanStack Query hook wrapping api/client.ts call
pattern_selection.web_data: Direct api/client.ts call in useEffect or custom hook

error_mapping.description: verify_firebase_token raises HTTPException when token verification fails
LLM client init raises error; check config.py environment variables
Supabase client returns error in .execute() response; routes should check and raise

stack.name: Firebase
stack.purpose: Frontend auth SDK + backend Admin token verification

stack.name: Axios
stack.purpose: HTTP client for web + mobile API calls

templates.description: FastAPI router with auth + DB injection
Async LangGraph node emitting SSE events
React page component with auth-protected route

technology.project_structure:
BedtimeApp/
├── package.json          # root monorepo scripts (concurrently)
├── package-lock.json
├── DOCS/                 # architecture docs, plans, schema
├── tuck-in-tales-backend/
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── main.py
│   │   ├── config.py
│   │   ├── routes/       # characters, stories, family, memories, prompts
│   │   ├── models/       # character, story, family, memory, user, prompt
│   │   ├── graphs/       # story_generator, avatar_generator, memory_analyzer
│   │   ├── services/     # family_service
│   │   └── utils/        # auth, sse, supabase, llm clients, prompt_resolver
│   └── tests/
├── tuck-in-tales-frontend/
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── App.tsx
│   │   ├── pages/        # all route-level page components
│   │   ├── components/   # ui/, Auth/, Layout/, prompts/
│   │   ├── hooks/        # useSSEStream, useStoryStream, etc.
│   │   ├── context/      # AuthContext
│   │   ├── models/       # TS interfaces mirroring backend
│   │   ├── api/          # client.ts
│   │   └── utils/        # firebase, supabaseUtils
│   └── _restore/         # legacy backend backup (do not modify)
└── tuck-in-tales-mobile/
    ├── App.tsx
    ├── app/              # Expo Router: (auth)/, (tabs)/, story/[id].tsx
    └── src/
        ├── api/          # client.ts
        ├── context/      # AuthContext
        ├── hooks/        # queries/, useGoogleSignIn
        ├── models/       # TS interfaces
        ├── config/       # firebase, supabase
        └── utils/        # supabaseUtils

run_commands.dev_all: npm run dev (root) — starts backend + web frontend concurrently
run_commands.dev_backend: cd tuck-in-tales-backend && poetry run uvicorn src.main:app --reload
run_commands.build_web: cd tuck-in-tales-frontend && npm run build (tsc -b && vite build)

frontend.rendering_strategy: CSR (web SPA via Vite) + Native compilation (mobile via Expo)

ui_components.name: AppLayout
ui_components.description: Authenticated route wrapper with Sidebar navigation

ui_components.name: ProtectedRoute
ui_components.description: Redirects unauthenticated users to /login using AuthContext

ui_components.name: CharacterList
ui_components.description: Grid of character cards fetched from backend /characters

ui_components.name: StoryProgressPage
ui_components.description: Real-time story generation viewer consuming useStoryStream SSE hook

ui_components.name: PromptEditor
ui_components.description: Admin UI for editing AI prompt templates stored in Supabase

ui_components.name: Sidebar
ui_components.description: Main nav with links to Characters, Stories, Memories, Family, Prompts

state_management.approach: React Context for auth; TanStack Query for server state; local useState for form/UI state
state_management.server_state: TanStack Query @tanstack/react-query v5 for mobile; direct axios calls + useState for web
state_management.local_state: React useState/useReducer in page components for forms and UI state
state_management.rationale: No Redux/Zustand needed; auth is the only cross-cutting global state

data_fetching.name: useSSEStream
data_fetching.mechanism: Browser EventSource API via custom hook
data_fetching.when_to_use: Any real-time streaming from backend (story, avatar, memory analysis)

data_fetching.name: TanStack Query (mobile)
data_fetching.when_to_use: All mobile data fetching with caching requirements

data_fetching.name: Direct Axios (web)
data_fetching.mechanism: api/client.ts Axios instance with Bearer token interceptor
data_fetching.when_to_use: Web CRUD operations; called from useEffect or event handlers

frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS utility-first + Radix UI unstyled primitives + shadcn/ui component patterns (CVA variants, clsx merging) — web only; mobile uses React Native native styles
key_conventions: Web UI components in components/ui/ follow shadcn/ui pattern with class-variance-authority
All page components go in src/pages/{Domain}Page.tsx
SSE hooks composed from useSSEStream.ts base with domain-specific wrappers
Mobile routes defined by file structure under app/ following Expo Router conventions
Auth token injected automatically by api/client.ts; never manually add Authorization headers

developer_recipes.task: Add a new AI-powered feature with streaming progress
steps: 1. Create tuck-in-tales-backend/src/graphs/{domain}_generator.py with LangGraph StateGraph; nodes call send_sse_event(client_id, 'chunk'/'status'/'done', data)
2. Add SSE route in tuck-in-tales-backend/src/routes/{domain}.py: POST to start generation, GET /{id}/stream returns StreamingResponse(sse_generator(id))
4. Create tuck-in-tales-frontend/src/hooks/use{Domain}Stream.ts extending useSSEStream.ts with domain-specific event handlers
5. Use hook in tuck-in-tales-frontend/src/pages/{Domain}Page.tsx to display real-time progress

developer_recipes.task: Add a new backend CRUD resource
steps: 2. Create route file in tuck-in-tales-backend/src/routes/{domain}.py with Depends(verify_firebase_token) and Depends(get_supabase_client) on each handler

developer_recipes.task: Add a new web page with protected route
steps: 1. Create tuck-in-tales-frontend/src/pages/{Domain}Page.tsx as a React functional component
2. Add route in tuck-in-tales-frontend/src/App.tsx inside the AppLayout ProtectedRoute wrapper: <Route path='/{domain}' element={<{Domain}Page />} />

developer_recipes.task: Add a new mobile tab screen
steps: 1. Create tuck-in-tales-mobile/app/(tabs)/{screen}.tsx as Expo Router screen component
3. Create data hook in tuck-in-tales-mobile/src/hooks/queries/use{Domain}.ts using TanStack Query useQuery wrapping api/client.ts

architecture_diagram:
graph TD FE[Web Frontend
React/Vite SPA] -->|REST + Bearer token| API MOB[Mobile App
React Native/Expo] -->|REST + Bearer token| API FE -->|EventSource SSE| SSE_ROUTE[SSE Endpoints] API[FastAPI Routes
routes/] --> AUTH[Auth Middleware
utils/auth.py] API --> GRAPHS[LangGraph Graphs
graphs/] API --> SVC[Service Layer
services/] AUTH --> FB[Firebase Admin SDK] AUTH --> SB[(Supabase PostgreSQL)] GRAPHS --> LLM[LLM Clients
OpenAI/Gemini/Groq] GRAPHS --> SSE_UTIL[SSE Utility
utils/sse.py] SSE_UTIL --> SSE_ROUTE GRAPHS --> PR[Prompt Resolver
utils/prompt_resolver.py] PR --> SB SVC --> SB API --> SB FE -->|Firebase SDK| FB_AUTH[Firebase Auth] MOB -->|Firebase SDK| FB_AUTH

pitfalls.description: If a LangGraph graph node raises an exception without calling send_sse_event(client_id, 'error', ...) first, the frontend EventSource in useSSEStream.ts will hang indefinitely waiting for a terminal event
pitfalls.recommendation: Wrap all graph nodes in try/except; always emit 'error' or 'done' event before re-raising. Check tuck-in-tales-backend/src/utils/sse.py terminal event handling.

pitfalls.description: verify_firebase_token in tuck-in-tales-backend/src/utils/auth.py calls get_or_create_supabase_user on every request; if Supabase is slow this adds latency to all authenticated calls
pitfalls.recommendation: Consider caching user lookups in-memory with TTL; or use Supabase's JWT verification directly to skip the extra DB call on subsequent requests

pitfalls.description: tuck-in-tales-frontend/_restore/backend-src/ contains legacy backend code that mirrors tuck-in-tales-backend/src/; changes to active backend are NOT reflected in _restore and vice versa
pitfalls.recommendation: Never import from _restore/; treat it as read-only historical reference. See tuck-in-tales-frontend/RESTORE-GUIDE.md for context.

pitfalls.description: Provider routing in story_generator.py uses if/elif on provider string from prompt config; adding a new provider requires editing graph files directly
pitfalls.recommendation: When adding a new LLM provider, update tuck-in-tales-backend/src/utils/ with a new client module AND update all if/elif chains in tuck-in-tales-backend/src/graphs/

pitfalls.area: Mobile vs web data fetching divergence
pitfalls.description: Mobile uses TanStack Query hooks (hooks/queries/) with caching; web uses direct axios calls with manual useState — same API, different caching semantics
pitfalls.recommendation: When adding a new API call, create TanStack Query hook for mobile in tuck-in-tales-mobile/src/hooks/queries/; for web consider adding useQuery wrapper for consistency

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: Frontend obtains Firebase ID token via auth.currentUser.getIdToken(); token sent as Bearer in Authorization header; backend Depends(verify_firebase_token) in auth.py validates via Firebase Admin SDK and provisions Supabase user on first login
tips: Token refresh is handled by Firebase SDK; use onAuthStateChanged for reactive updates
api/client.ts on both platforms injects token automatically; never add Authorization manually
get_or_create_supabase_user() is called per request; profile data is in Supabase, not Firebase

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: asyncio.Queue registry in sse.py keyed by client_id; graph nodes call send_sse_event(id, event, data); route exposes GET /{id}/stream returning StreamingResponse(sse_generator(id)); frontend useSSEStream.ts opens EventSource and dispatches named events
tips: client_id must match between graph state and frontend hook parameter (typically story_id or character_id)
Always emit terminal event ('done'/'error') from graph nodes to close EventSource
SSE is unidirectional; use REST for any client→server communication during streaming

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: StateGraph compiled with async nodes; state flows as TypedDict; nodes invoke LLM clients, emit SSE events, update Supabase; graph invoked from route handlers via compiled_graph.stream(initial_state)
implementation_guidelines.usage_example: workflow = StateGraph(StoryState); workflow.add_node('generate', node_fn); app = workflow.compile()
tips: Use asyncio.to_thread() for sync Supabase client calls within async nodes
State TypedDict must be defined before graph compilation
Emit SSE status events at start of each node for frontend progress indication

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: Separate client modules per provider; IMAGE_GENERATION_PROVIDER env var in config.py selects provider at runtime; graphs use if/elif to route to correct client; FALLBACK_DEFAULTS in prompt_resolver.py specify per-prompt provider
tips: All API keys loaded via pydantic-settings BaseSettings in config.py from environment
Missing optional provider keys (GROQ_API_KEY) won't break startup; only fail at call time
Avatar uses Gemini vision for analysis; OpenAI gpt-image-1 for generation by default

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: resolve_prompt(key, variables, supabase) in prompt_resolver.py fetches prompt config from Supabase 'prompts' table, caches with TTL, performs ${variable} substitution; falls back to FALLBACK_DEFAULTS dict if DB row missing
tips: PromptEditor/PromptPlayground in web frontend allows live editing of prompt templates in Supabase
FALLBACK_DEFAULTS in prompt_resolver.py must be updated when adding new prompt keys
Cache TTL is ~60s; changes to prompts in Supabase are reflected within 1 minute

development_rules.rule: Always use FastAPI Depends(verify_firebase_token) and Depends(get_supabase_client) on every protected route handler — never access Firebase or Supabase directly in route function bodies without dependency injection
Always emit a terminal SSE event ('done' or 'error') from every LangGraph graph node execution path — never leave an SSE queue open without a terminal signal
Never hardcode AI provider API keys or Firebase credentials — all secrets must be loaded via pydantic-settings BaseSettings in config.py from environment variables
Never import from or modify tuck-in-tales-frontend/_restore/ — it is a read-only legacy backup; all active backend code lives exclusively in tuck-in-tales-backend/src/
Backend tests use pytest with fixtures defined in conftest.py — always add new route tests to tuck-in-tales-backend/tests/routes/ following the pattern in test_characters.py

When adding a new LangGraph AI workflow, mirror domain model names exactly between tuck-in-tales-backend/src/models/{domain}.py (Pydantic) and tuck-in-tales-frontend/src/models/{domain}.ts (TypeScript interface)

deployment.runtime_environment: Backend: Python/Uvicorn server (Poetry); Web: Browser SPA (Vite build); Mobile: iOS/Android native via Expo
deployment.serverless_functions: Firebase Cloud Functions capability available (Firebase dependency) but not implemented in this codebase
distribution: Web: Vite static build output for CDN/static hosting
Mobile: Expo build (EAS) for App Store / Google Play
deployment.environment_config: Backend: .env loaded via pydantic-settings (FIREBASE_CREDENTIALS, SUPABASE_URL/KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, GROQ_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY); Web: VITE_FIREBASE_*, VITE_BACKEND_URL; Mobile: EXPO_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_*, EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL
